title: buildah image v1.34: Error: open /usr/lib/containers/storage/overlay-images/images.lock: permission denied
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Description**
When run with Docker (haven't tested Podman) and our security configs, buildah image v1.34 throws an error on most operations. No previous versions have this issue. Although I submitted the report for an Ubuntu system, I observed the exact same behavior on Centos. We use the Podman `seccomp.json` and the following AppArmor policy:
```
#include <tunables/global>

profile docker-buildah flags=(attach_disconnected,mediate_deleted) {
  #include <abstractions/base>

  network,
  capability,
  file,
  umount,
  # The buildah container requires mount
  mount,
  # Host (privileged) processes may send signals to container processes.
  signal (receive) peer=unconfined,
  # dockerd may send signals to container processes (for "docker kill").
  signal (receive) peer=/usr/bin/docker,
  # Container processes may send signals amongst themselves.
  signal (send,receive) peer=docker-buildah,

  deny @{PROC}/* w,   # deny write for all files directly in /proc (not in a subdir)
  # deny write to files not in /proc/<number>/** or /proc/sys/**
  deny @{PROC}/{[^1-9],[^1-9][^0-9],[^1-9s][^0-9y][^0-9s],[^1-9][^0-9][^0-9][^0-9/]*}/** w,
  deny @{PROC}/sys/[^k]** w,  # deny /proc/sys except /proc/sys/k* (effectively /proc/sys/kernel)
  deny @{PROC}/sys/kernel/{?,??,[^s][^h][^m]**} w,  # deny everything except shm* in /proc/sys/kernel/
  deny @{PROC}/sysrq-trigger rwklx,
  deny @{PROC}/kcore rwklx,

  deny /sys/[^f]*/** wklx,
  deny /sys/f[^s]*/** wklx,
  deny /sys/fs/[^c]*/** wklx,
  deny /sys/fs/c[^g]*/** wklx,
  deny /sys/fs/cg[^r]*/** wklx,
  deny /sys/firmware/** rwklx,
  deny /sys/devices/virtual/powercap/** rwklx,
  deny /sys/kernel/security/** rwklx,

  # suppress ptrace denials when using 'docker ps' or using 'ps' inside a container
  ptrace (trace,read,tracedby,readby) peer=docker-buildah,
}
```
<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. `docker run -t --device /dev/fuse  --security-opt seccomp=/usr/share/containers/seccomp.json --security-opt apparmor=docker-buildah -u build quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.34 buildah pull fedora`


**Describe the results you received:**
buildah image v1.34 throws the following error when pulling, building, etc.:
```
docker run -t --device /dev/fuse  --security-opt seccomp=/usr/share/containers/seccomp.json --security-opt apparmor=docker-buildah -u build quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.34 buildah pull fedora
Error: open /usr/lib/containers/storage/overlay-images/images.lock: permission denied
WARN[0000] failed to shutdown storage: "open /usr/lib/containers/storage/overlay-images/images.lock: permission denied"
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Buildah operations complete successfully  when I use v1.33.2 or earlier:

```
docker run -t --device /dev/fuse  --security-opt seccomp=/usr/share/containers/seccomp.json --security-opt apparmor=docker-buildah -u build quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.33.2 buildah pull fedora
Resolved "fedora" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 718a00fe3212 done   |
Copying config 368a084ba1 done   |
Writing manifest to image destination
```

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
N/A
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
N/A
```

**Output of `podman version` if reporting a `podman build` issue:**

```
N/A
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
DISTRIB_ID=Ubuntu
DISTRIB_RELEASE=22.04
DISTRIB_CODENAME=jammy
DISTRIB_DESCRIPTION="Ubuntu 22.04.3 LTS"
PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 22.04.3 LTS"
NAME="Ubuntu"
VERSION_ID="22.04"
VERSION="22.04.3 LTS (Jammy Jellyfish)"
VERSION_CODENAME=jammy
ID=ubuntu
ID_LIKE=debian
HOME_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://help.ubuntu.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/"
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/legal/terms-and-policies/privacy-policy"
UBUNTU_CODENAME=jammy
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux ****.**************.com 5.15.0-76-generic #83-Ubuntu SMP Thu Jun 15 19:16:32 UTC 2023 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
N/A
```

comments.body:
We have the same issue, seems like a regression after upgrading to `v1.34.0.` (upgraded from `v1.32.2` where everything runs well)
We run on `k8s` and we get 
`Error: open /usr/lib/containers/storage/overlay-images/images.lock: permission denied`

Any Advice? 
Do we need to set an `EmptyDir' over this path?

@zmjackson Does it work if you add `--privileged --security-opt seccomp=unconfined --security-opt apparmor=unconfined` ? Just to make sure that issue is not with the new profiles ? if it works we can work to figure out what is causing the issue.
I just checked and I get the same error when using `--privileged --security-opt seccomp=unconfined --security-opt apparmor=unconfined`.

@zmjackson just changed the security context from

```
container:
    securityContext:
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
      runAsUser: 1000
      runAsGroup: 1000
      capabilities:
        drop:
          - ALL
```

to 
```
securityContext:
   priviledged: true
```

and it worked...but as you know we don't want to run `buildah` with these kind of priviledges
@zmjackson what do you think about that ^^ any ideas how we can proceed?
@flouthoc or @rhatdan thoughts on what might be tweaked to fix this?

Seems to be caused by the configuration of the container storage library.

Buildah 1.27 do not have a storage.conf in $HOME/.config/containers/storage but  Buildah 1.34 does.

I did a test without the storage.conf in $HOME and it almost works excepted some warnings not present with v1.27.

I ran minikube with podman rootless for the tests. The storage is configured with the kernel overlay and not the fuse one.

```

/home/build/.config/containers
```

```
sylvain@debian12:~$ cat buildah-latest2.yaml 
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
 name: buildah-latestfixed
spec:
 containers:
   - name: buildah
     image: quay.io/buildah/stable:latest
     args:
       - sleep
       - "1000000"
     securityContext:
       runAsUser: 1000
```

@sylvainpelletier thanks,
 is there a reason for the `storage.conf ` to be added, is there a way to have the previous behaviour like at 1.27 ?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@zmjackson FYI, 
After adding the Buildha POD and EmpytDir mounted over `/usr/lib/containers/storage/overlay-images` it fixes the problem.
Though I still see error (which are then fall back) for set UID and GID, and I could not resolved with given capabilities 
but it least it can work
```yaml
container:
        securityContext:
          allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
          runAsUser: 1000
          runAsGroup: 1000
          capabilities:
            drop:
              - ALL
        image: "quay.io/containers/buildah:v1.35.3"
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent       
        volumeMounts:
           - name: buildah-storage
              mountPath: /usr/lib/containers/storage/overlay-images
volumes:
   - name: buildah-storage
      emptyDir: {}

```

Getting the same issue with v1.38.1

```
[build@9472efe26bdc ~]$ buildah build -t test:1 .
Error: creating lock file directory: mkdir /usr/lib/containers/storage/vfs-images: permission denied
WARN[0000] failed to shutdown storage: "creating lock file directory: mkdir /usr/lib/containers/storage/vfs-images: permission denied"
```

Works fine with v1.23.1

```
[build@f1d1c919fb5b ~]$ buildah build -t test:1 .
STEP 1/4: FROM alpine:3
Resolved "alpine" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:3...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 6e771e15690e done
Copying config 8d591b0b7d done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2/4: RUN adduser -D default
STEP 3/4: USER default
STEP 4/4: WORKDIR /home/default
COMMIT test:1
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob a16e98724c05 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 2320c316f0c0 done
Copying config 122b028de3 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> 122b028de31
Successfully tagged localhost/test:1
122b028de31f67869640576a8463602ec716fe78b23f3659807dadfba84e7dd8
```

Still an issue in 1.39.0

```
buildah pull iad.ocir.io/XXXXXXX/devops/ado-agent:v2.0.0
========================== Starting Command Output ===========================
/usr/bin/bash /azp/_work/_temp/a5b1864b-3334-44ba-9aaf-8ad1bd6cfb20.sh
Error: open /usr/lib/containers/storage/overlay-images/images.lock: permission denied
time="2025-05-27T18:23:36Z" level=warning msg="failed to shutdown storage: \"open /usr/lib/containers/storage/overlay-images/images.lock: permission denied\""
```

```
buildah run $(buildah from alpine) env
========================== Starting Command Output ===========================
/usr/bin/bash /azp/_work/_temp/7cc5b9ff-cfa3-4bcc-808a-a396d6b3d7e0.sh
Error: open /usr/lib/containers/storage/overlay-images/images.lock: permission denied
time="2025-05-27T18:23:25Z" level=warning msg="failed to shutdown storage: \"open /usr/lib/containers/storage/overlay-images/images.lock: permission denied\""
```

In my case, what helped was specifying the overlay storage driver explicitly by running `buildah --storage-driver=overlay bud`.

I don't know why this is required, though, as `buildah --help` prints `--storage-driver string storage-driver (default "overlay")` and it's also configured in `~/.config/containers/storage.conf` in the container.


For context, I'm running `quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.40.1` on Kubernetes with the following Pod manifest (`kubectl create -f pod.yaml`):
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  generateName: testbuildah-
  namespace: buildah
spec:
  containers:
    - name: buildah
      image: quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.40.1
      stdin: true
      tty: true
      resources:
        limits:
          nextflow.io/fuse: 1
        requests:
          cpu: 100m
          memory: 200Mi
      securityContext:
        allowPrivilegeEscalation: true
        seccompProfile:
          type: Unconfined
        appArmorProfile:
          type: Unconfined
        capabilities:
          add:
            - SETFCAP
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 1000
  restartPolicy: Never
```
I'm using the [FUSE device plugin by nextflow](https://github.com/nextflow-io/k8s-fuse-plugin) to provide `/dev/fuse` without having to give the buildah container root privileges.

@sysedwinistrator your insight led me to trying to change the my storage driver as well.  If I add `--storage-driver=vfs` to my `buildah` commands, my errors go away.  I have not done any custom configuration, as far as I know. The storage.conf file shows it should be overlay

buildah is 1.40.1, host OS is Oracle Linux 8.10

Setting the storage driver for each call wasn't going to work out well for us, so we opted to remove the storage.conf from the build users home directory as updates to it didn't seem to have any affect. So far, no issues.

This also fixed it for me:
```
rm ~/.config/containers/storage.conf || echo no user storage.conf to remove
```
prior to running `buildah` in a GitLab CI pipeline.

I am running buildah rootless from the quay image via GitLab config yaml:
```
  image:
    name: quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.41.5
    docker:
      user:  build
```

I hit this issue when I started running buildah as the `build` user from this image.

> In my case, what helped was specifying the overlay storage driver explicitly by running `buildah --storage-driver=overlay bud`.

I can confirm that.

In fact in v1.34 `/etc/containers/storage.conf` has been modified:

```diff
  [storage.options]
  additionalimagestores = [
  "/var/lib/shared",
+ "/usr/lib/containers/storage",
  ]
```

idem for `/home/build/.config/containers/storage.conf`:

```diff
# Must be comma separated list.
  [storage.options]
  additionalimagestores = [
  "/var/lib/shared",
+ "/usr/lib/containers/storage",
  ]
```

but `/usr/lib/containers/storage` subdirectory are created with mode 0700:

```bash
# ls -al /usr/lib/containers/storage
total 16
drwxr-xr-x 4 root root 4096 Mar 15  2024 .
drwxr-xr-x 3 root root 4096 Mar 15  2024 ..
drwx------ 2 root root 4096 Mar 15  2024 overlay-images
drwx------ 2 root root 4096 Mar 15  2024 overlay-layer
```

So `/usr/lib/containers/storage/overlay-images/images.lock` cannot be read by the `build` user.

Either removing `/usr/lib/containers/storage` from `additionalimagestores` in `storage.conf` files  
 or `chmod a+rx /usr/lib/containers/storage/overlay-images/` solves the problem.

If using `vfs` storage driver then `/usr/lib/containers/storage/vf-images` and `/usr/lib/containers/storage/vf-layers` are missing,
but `buildah` still try to read `/usr/lib/containers/storage/vf-images/images.lock` which fails.

Creating `/usr/lib/containers/storage/vf-images/images.lock` and `/usr/lib/containers/storage/vf-layer/layers.lock` with read access for everyone, can also solve the issue.
